name: Roman Road Trail - Stage 3
name_original: Camino de Don Benito a Villanueva de la Serena — circular por la Vereda
description: Jog the moderate 5.7-mile Roman Road Trail - Stage 3, gaining 206 feet in 1 hour 4 minutes, connecting Don Benito to Villanueva de la Serena
region_intro: Jogging around Don Benito offers diverse landscapes for outdoor activity. The region features a mix of natural paths, municipal parks, and rural areas. Terrain includes river valleys, Mediterranean forests, and holm oak pastures, providing varied running environments. The area is generally characterized by gentle elevations, with some routes exploring more undulating terrain. Best jogging routes around Don Benito The most popular jogging route is Roman Road Trail - Stage 3, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete. This route utilizes an old railway line, offering an accessible and traffic-free path. Another top favourite among local runners is Alto de la Ortiga Loop Trail, a difficult 12.4 miles (19.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, exploring areas near the Ortiga river and granite rock formations. Local runners also love the Sierra de la Utrera and Castillo de, a 14.2 miles (22.9 km) trail leading through varied natural scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Jogging around Don Benito is defined by natural paths, municipal parks, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy park loops to more challenging trails with notable elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Don Benito's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy running routes in Don Benito suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Don Benito offers several accessible options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283220584/roman-road-trail-stage-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road Trail - Stage 3&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old railway line, is an easy 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path, free from motorized traffic and ideal for a gentle run. Additionally, local parks like &lt;strong&gt;Parque Municipal "Las Albercas"&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Enrique Tierno Galván Park&lt;/strong&gt; provide pleasant, flat environments for easy jogging.

Q: What are some challenging or long-distance running trails around Don Benito?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284016642/alto-de-la-ortiga-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Ortiga Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.4 miles (19.9 km) route with significant elevation gain, exploring areas near the Ortiga river and granite rock formations. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283320997/sierra-de-la-utrera-and-castillo-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Utrera and Castillo de&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.2 miles (22.9 km) trail through varied natural scenery.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Don Benito?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito loop from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.7 km (5.4 miles) circular route that starts and ends in the town. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284009405/circular-sierra-de-la-gangosa-and-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Sierra de la Gangosa and Río&lt;/a&gt; also offers a challenging 22.9 km (14.2 miles) loop through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging paths in Don Benito?
A: Yes, several areas are great for families. The &lt;strong&gt;Vereda de Don Benito a Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/strong&gt;, an old railway line, is flat and traffic-free, making it safe for all ages. Parks like &lt;strong&gt;Parque Municipal "Las Albercas"&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Enrique Tierno Galván Park&lt;/strong&gt; also provide spacious, green environments perfect for a family run or walk.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on running routes in Don Benito?
A: Don Benito's routes offer diverse scenery. You might encounter the artificial lake at &lt;strong&gt;Parque Municipal "Las Albercas"&lt;/strong&gt;, the unique palm trees of &lt;strong&gt;El Palmeral&lt;/strong&gt;, or the Mediterranean forests and granite rock formations near &lt;strong&gt;La Serrezuela&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Paraje Natural de Santa Natalia&lt;/strong&gt;, about 15 km from town, features a waterfall during rainy seasons and the historic "Puente de Santa Natalia" over the Guadámez river. You can also explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt; in the wider region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Don Benito?
A: Don Benito experiences a temperate climate with mild winters, making it suitable for running for much of the year. While summers can be hot, especially midday, early mornings or late evenings are pleasant. Spring and autumn offer ideal conditions with comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural scenery.

Q: How many running routes are available in Don Benito?
A: There are over 40 dedicated running routes around Don Benito documented on komoot, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulties to suit all preferences. This includes 2 easy routes, 30 moderate, and 9 difficult trails.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines in Don Benito?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Vereda de Don Benito a Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/strong&gt; is a 4.3-kilometer natural path that utilizes an old railway line. It's an excellent option for running, offering an accessible and traffic-free experience between the two towns.

Q: Are there any specific parks in Don Benito that are good for jogging?
A: Certainly. &lt;strong&gt;Parque Municipal "Las Albercas"&lt;/strong&gt; is a spacious park with an artificial lake, perfect for a leisurely run. &lt;strong&gt;Enrique Tierno Galván Park&lt;/strong&gt;, covering 20.8 acres, also provides ample space for walking and jogging within the town. The &lt;strong&gt;Bulevar de la Avenida del Pilar&lt;/strong&gt;, with its impressive collection of palm and cinamomo trees, offers another pleasant green zone for urban runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Don Benito?
A: The terrain around Don Benito is quite varied. You'll find flat, natural paths like the old railway line, as well as more undulating routes through Mediterranean forests, holm oak pastures, and areas with granite rock formations, particularly in places like &lt;strong&gt;La Serrezuela&lt;/strong&gt;. Some trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284016642/alto-de-la-ortiga-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Ortiga Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, feature significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Don Benito?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the diverse landscapes and the variety of routes available in Don Benito. Runners appreciate the mix of accessible park paths for easy runs and more challenging trails with natural beauty, such as those exploring river valleys and forests. Over 200 runners have used komoot to explore the region's varied terrain, highlighting its appeal for outdoor exercise.
tips: Beautiful path that connects both towns and is constantly surrounded by agricultural fields. Depending on the time of year, the colors of the fields give an impressive horizon!

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on Roman Road Trail - Stage 3?
faqs.answer: The trail generally features a dirt/gravel surface, characteristic of ancient Roman roads designed for durability. While often firm and well-drained, sections can become muddy, especially after rain. You might also encounter raised embankments (aggers) and lateral ditches, remnants of Roman engineering.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog this route?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is approximately 9.12 kilometers long and typically takes about 1 hour and 4 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is the Roman Road Trail - Stage 3 suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: With a 'moderate' difficulty grade and an elevation gain of around 63 meters, this trail is generally suitable for joggers with some experience. While Roman roads were often flat, this stage includes slight elevation changes that contribute to its moderate rating.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight along this stage is the Path from Don Benito to Villanueva de la Serena. You'll be jogging on paths with historical significance, potentially through diverse scenery including forests, fields, or wooded areas, connecting you to ancient Roman engineering.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog policies for this particular stage is not available. However, many trails on repurposed Roman roads are generally accessible for dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this trail depends on local weather conditions. Given that sections can become muddy, dry seasons are generally preferable. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures for jogging, while summer might be hot and winter could bring mud or colder conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to access Roman Road Trail - Stage 3. Most repurposed Roman roads are public access routes.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is named "Roman Road Trail - Stage 3" and is described as a path from Don Benito to Villanueva de la Serena. Specific trailhead parking information is not provided in the available data. It is advisable to research local parking options in Don Benito or Villanueva de la Serena before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: While specific viewpoints are not detailed, the historical significance of jogging on an ancient Roman road is a landmark in itself. You'll be following in the footsteps of ancient Romans, and some sections may even feature preserved paving stones or other historical elements.

faqs.question: What should I pack for jogging on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the dirt/gravel terrain and potential for mud, sturdy jogging shoes with good grip are recommended. Always carry water, especially on longer runs. Depending on the season, consider layers of clothing, sun protection, and a basic first-aid kit.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino Romano - Etapa 3, covering approximately 71% of this official trail.

waypoints.name: Path from Don Benito to Villanueva de la Serena
